authorization: Authorize S10-D2 terminal-lesson sealed report only.

candidate.strategy_class: cross-asset Donchian breakout, no-pyramid, reparameterised capital

design_rationale: Path B chosen: record the S10-D2 terminal lesson as a sealed report under reports/external_research_hunter/ rather than appending to brain_memory/projects/trading_bot/lessons.md. This preserves the no-lessons-md invariant that has held across every commit in this controller session.

discipline_lesson.full_text: At the moment of the P10 OOS verdict, three temptations existed and were all rejected: (1) lowering K9 from 100 to e.g. 50 'because the direction is encouraging' -- rejected (P11 explicit_blocked_actions: 'DO NOT relax K9 threshold to confirm OOS'); (2) waving the qualitative result through as a 'soft confirmation' because all OOS metrics were positive -- rejected (qualitative verdict explicitly enum-bounded to OOS_INDETERMINATE_BUT_DIRECTIONALLY_CONSISTENT, not OOS_CONFIRMED); (3) advancing to a longer-OOS rerun by quietly re-using the existing chain artifacts as priors without a fresh authorization -- rejected (each future option requires separate sealed authorization). The discipline that held was: pre-anchored verdict enums (C7 has 3 fixed values; P11 has 4 fixed values), pre-anchored thresholds (K9 = 100 was set in Tier-N spec long before P10 ran), and the cultural rule that 'lacking statistical confirmation is not a basis for advancement'.
discipline_lesson.one_sentence: The framework correctly parked instead of weakening K9 or claiming confirmation.
discipline_lesson.why_this_matters_long_term: Each time the framework refuses to loosen a threshold or widen an enum, it accumulates trust. Every candidate that gets a fair conservative verdict makes the next candidate's verdict more credible. Conversely, one threshold-loosening event would invalidate the chain-of-custody discipline for every prior verdict (s7-D1 reject, s8-D1 park, s9 park, s10-D1 park, s10-D2 park) and call into question whether any future PASS verdict actually means PASS.

future_research_options_listed_but_not_a.meta: NONE of these options is pre-approved. Each requires a separate fresh sealed operator authorization. Listing here is purely informational.

key_is_evidence.source: P6 IS diagnostic at commit d6977631 (seal e6cdc7c68a9e2b7b)

main_lesson.full_text: S10-D2 is the first multi-phase Tier-N candidate in this research stream to clear every IS-side gate cleanly (IS performance, full cost-stress matrix S0-S3, K10 universe diversification, all safety counters zero, 0 K-fires on IS) and produce a directionally-consistent OOS result. It is NOT a rejected fake edge: the OOS evidence pointed the same way as IS on every observable metric (positive sharpe, positive expectancy, positive net PnL, drawdown actually improved, safety counters still zero). It is also NOT a confirmed live edge: the OOS sample of 53 closed trades over the 3-year OOS window was below the K9 threshold of 100 trades that the framework requires to convert directional evidence into statistical confirmation. The correct epistemic label is 'promising but statistically under-sampled'.
main_lesson.one_sentence: This was not a rejected fake edge and not a confirmed live edge; it is a promising but statistically under-sampled candidate.
main_lesson.why_this_framing_matters: Calling this a 'fake edge' (rejection) would discard the cleanest end-to-end research lifecycle in the stream. Calling this a 'confirmed edge' (advancement) would require loosening K9, which the framework explicitly forbids. The third label -- promising but under-sampled -- is the only honest reading.

no_brokerage_connection.reason: explicit_blocked_actions includes DO NOT call Databento API, QuantConnect, or any broker/exchange/wallet API

no_idea_memory_mutation.reason: idea_memory is production-side; PARKED research candidates do not write to it

no_live_trading.reason: permanent invariant; cannot be lifted by any research verdict

no_paper_trading.reason: verdict_never_means_live_ready; no broker / paper-trade route exists for PARKED candidates

no_review_queue_mutation.reason: review_queue.json is a Strategy Lab operational artifact; PARKED candidates are not queued for trading review; P11 explicit_blocked_actions includes DO NOT mutate review_queue.json

no_strategy_lab_promotion.reason: Strategy Lab promotion requires FRC + CONFIRMED OOS verdict + live-readiness, none of which exist for S10-D2

seal_method: sha256 over json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=False) EXCLUDING report_seal_sha256 + seal_method

structural_lesson.full_text: The binding constraint that prevented S10-D2 from earning a CONFIRMED OOS verdict was the available OOS history length (3 years from 2023-01-01 to 2025-12-31). At a per-year trade frequency of 17.7 trades/year (preserved at 88% of the IS rate, confirming the s8-D1 sizing-fix lesson worked), a 3-year window yielded 53 trades -- structurally below the 100-trade K9 threshold. Cost-stress was NOT the binding constraint (S0-S3 all positive, K12 did not fire). K10 was NOT the binding constraint (avg pairwise correlation 0.05 << 0.50 threshold). Safety was NOT the binding constraint (all counters held zero across IS + 5 cost tiers + OOS). The constraint was simply 'not enough history yet'.
structural_lesson.one_sentence: OOS window length / sample size was the binding constraint, not cost-stress, not K10, not safety.
structural_lesson.operational_implication: Future Tier-N candidates with similar trade-frequency characteristics (~20 trades/year) need an OOS window of >= 5 years to comfortably clear K9 at S1. For 3-year OOS windows, only candidates with > 33 trades/year per market will reliably clear K9. This is a calibration insight about Tier-N candidate design relative to available history.
structural_lesson.s8_d1_relationship: S8-D1 parked because sizing produced 0 contracts for most signals at $100,000 starting cash. S10-D2 fixed that by raising to $500,000 starting cash. The S10-D2 trade-density ratio of 0.88x (OOS/IS) confirms the sizing fix worked. The fix opened a different downstream constraint (OOS window length), which is a structurally different problem.
